McLean’s season-long skill is real, but his recent control slide is a bad fit against this lineup, and even with home field I make Atlanta 55.5% to win at a plus price.

Houston gets the better lineup split, the better bullpen, and the less troubling starter once both pitchers are regressed toward their broader skill indicators. I still come up a bit short of the threshold, and the suspect total market keeps me away from derivative exposure.

Roupp has the better underlying profile, but his recent control slippage and San Francisco’s poor bullpen keep the Giants from separating enough. Chicago’s bullpen is shaky too, so I do not see a clean late-game edge either way.

best_bet.why_this: Atlanta combines the biggest lineup advantage with the best bullpen edge among the playable sides, and the market is still hanging a plus price. My projected win probability is 55.5%, comfortably above the 47.6% implied by +110.
